title: URIs, URL Resolution, Origins, DNS, and Destination Identity · Automation and Data Client Systems · Python Build Room
summary: A civic-data automation team integrating a staging catalog must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for uris, url resolution, origins, dns, and destination identity, producing destination-resolution and origin contract. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers destination-resolution and origin contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Parse scheme, authority, host, port, path, query, and fragment and resolve relative references against the correct base. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Encode path segments and query names or values at the correct structural layer without double encoding. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Compare origins from scheme, host, and effective port while separating URI authority, Host, and connection address. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Uri Components Resolution
steps.instruction:
workshop case — civic-data staging importer receives base https://api5.example.test/v1/items/ and reference ../reports?q=a%2Fb. Resolve it structurally, compare origin and authority, trace two DNS answers, and reject any connected destination outside the approved range. Competency probe: Parse scheme, authority, host, port, path, query, and fragment and resolve relative references against the correct base. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Uri Components Resolution.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for parse scheme, authority, host, port, path, query, and fragment and resolve relative references against the correct base. demonstrate the result through python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports. For Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Uri Components Resolution, keep Uri Components Resolution connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

Competency probe: Canonicalize and validate schemes, hosts, IP literals, ports, IDNs, and resolved destinations before granting network authority. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
